steps.title: Identify what each choice is testing
steps.content:
When a transversal crosses two lines, the lines are parallel if and only if one of these holds:
  • Corresponding angles are equal
  • Alternate interior angles are equal
  • Co-interior angles (same-side interior) sum to 180°
Let me evaluate what each choice establishes:
Choice A: $x = 34°$ means $w + x = 146 + 34 = 180°$. These are angles at the same intersection forming a linear pair. This says nothing about the relationship between the two intersections. Not sufficient.
Choice D: $y + z = 180°$ means angles $y$ and $z$ are at the same right intersection forming a linear pair. Like choice A, this does not relate the two intersections. Not sufficient.

steps.title: Test whether B establishes equal corresponding angles
steps.content:
In the diagram, $w$ is at the left intersection and $y$ is at the right intersection.

Looking at their positions: $w$ is in the upper region where the transversal meets line $r$, and $y$ is in the upper region where the transversal meets line $s$.

These are corresponding angles—they occupy the same relative position at each intersection.
If $y = 146°$, then $w = y = 146°$, so the corresponding angles are equal.

By the converse of the Corresponding Angles Postulate, this proves $r \parallel s$. Choice B is sufficient.

steps.title: Verify that choice B is the answer
steps.content: To double-check, if $y = 146°$ and $w = 146°$, these equal corresponding angles guarantee that the two lines cut by the transversal are parallel. That matches choice B — B is correct.
